components.name: z3_solver
components.description: Z3 SMT solver: proof search, SAT/UNSAT determination, proof production
mitigations: SMT-LIB2 export enables cross-solver replay (CVC5, Yices)
QF_LIA fragment is decidable and well-tested
SAT models verified by direct substitution (self-certifying)
Alethe proof extraction provides independently checkable proofs
Z3 is mature (~15 years), widely used in industry and academia

components.name: z3_python_bindings
components.description: Z3 Python API: solver interface, model extraction, proof access
mitigations: Official Z3 bindings, maintained by Microsoft Research

components.name: pattern_database
components.description: 137 litmus test patterns with operations, forbidden outcomes, and dependencies
guarantees_affected: Pattern coverage (137 patterns cover important idioms)
mitigations: 228/228 herd7 agreement validates CPU pattern outcomes
Derived from published litmus test specifications

components.name: enumeration_engine
components.description: RF×CO exhaustive enumeration with ghb acyclicity checking

components.name: smt_encoding
components.description: Translation of litmus tests + memory models to QF_LIA SMT formulas
guarantees_affected: SMT formula faithfully represents the memory model
UNSAT proofs correspond to actual safety
SAT witnesses correspond to actual violations
mitigations: Full SMT-LIB2 specification documented in paper
Encoding follows standard axiomatic framework (Alglave et al.)

components.name: dsl_interpreter
components.description: Custom memory model DSL: parser, interpreter, fence selection
guarantees_affected: Custom model results match user intent

components.name: ast_analyzer
components.description: tree-sitter AST parsing, pattern matching, coverage confidence
mitigations: Does not affect formal verification (back-end is verified)
Users can manually specify patterns to bypass AST analysis

components.name: model_definitions
components.description: Built-in x86, SPARC, ARM, RISC-V, GPU model specifications
mitigations: Derived from published specifications (Owens et al., Pulte et al., RISC-V ISA)

components.name: compositional_reasoning
components.description: Multi-pattern composition: disjoint, SC-shared, Owicki-Gries, RG

components.name: severity_classification
components.description: CWE-calibrated severity taxonomy: data_race, security, benign

components.name: certificate_extractor
components.description: SMT-LIB2 file generation, Alethe proof extraction
mitigations: SAT models verified by direct substitution
Alethe proofs checkable by external validators

guarantees.description: If LITMUS∞ reports SAFE, the forbidden outcome is truly unreachable under the model
guarantees.mitigation: SMT-LIB2 cross-solver replay + Alethe proof extraction + QF_LIA decidability

guarantees.description: If LITMUS∞ reports UNSAFE, a concrete violating execution exists
guarantees.mitigation: SAT models self-certifying by direct substitution; 228/228 cross-validation

guarantees.description: Source code correctly identified as matching a litmus test pattern
guarantees.mitigation: 238-snippet benchmark; unverified front-end does not affect back-end guarantees
Paper proofs only; conservative fallback ensures no false negatives

guarantees.description: Unsafe pairs correctly classified by severity
guarantees.mitigation: Rule-based and CWE-calibrated; deterministic and auditable

guarantees.description: Recommended fences are minimal and sufficient
guarantees.mitigation: 99 UNSAT fence proofs; argmin construction trivially correct
Independent validation pathway against established tool

acknowledged_tcb: Z3 solver (external, ~500K LoC): all formal results depend on Z3 correctness
Python encoding logic (~1500 LoC): SMT formula construction
Pattern database (~2900 LoC): forbidden outcomes and model definitions
AST analyzer (~2100 LoC): code recognition (does not affect formal guarantees)
DSL interpreter (~470 LoC): custom model parsing
Compositional reasoning (~1760 LoC): paper proofs only
mitigations_in_place: SMT-LIB2 export: enables cross-solver validation (Z3, CVC5, Yices)
Alethe proof extraction: independently checkable UNSAT proofs
herd7 cross-validation: 228/228 agreement for CPU models
GPU external validation: 94/94 against published literature
Denotational semantics: formal meaning for DSL constructs
QF_LIA decidability: fragment is well-tested across all solvers
remaining_gaps: Compositional theorems (Thms 4-7) are paper proofs, not mechanized
AST analyzer has no formal correctness guarantee
No adversarial testing of pattern database correctness
Severity taxonomy not validated against real CVEs
Z3 proof production mode may not be exercised in all code paths
